questions.question: How many twisted pairs are in a UTP Ethernet cable, and how many wires total?
questions.explanation: UTP (Unshielded Twisted Pair) is a copper Ethernet cable with 4 twisted pairs (8 wires); the twist helps protect against EMI.

questions.question: Which cable type is used between like devices such as switch-to-switch or router-to-router (pre Auto MDI-X)?
questions.explanation: A crossover cable has pairs reversed end-to-end (1 to 3, 2 to 6) and is used between like devices (switch-switch, router-router).

questions.question: What does SFP stand for?
questions.explanation: SFP stands for Small Form-factor Pluggable — a module inserted into a switch/router interface to connect a fiber-optic cable.

questions.question: What does EMI stand for?
questions.explanation: EMI stands for Electromagnetic Interference; the twists in UTP help protect signals against EMI.

questions.question: Which fiber type has a wider core allowing multiple light modes and uses cheaper LED transmitters for shorter distances?
questions.explanation: Multimode fiber has a wider core that allows multiple light modes; it supports shorter distances than single-mode and uses cheaper LED-based transmitters.

questions.question: What is the maximum distance for 10GBASE-LR?
questions.explanation: 10GBASE-LR supports 10 Gbps over single-mode fiber up to 10 km. 10GBASE-SR is multimode up to 400 m; 10GBASE-ER is single-mode up to 30 km.

questions.question: Which Ethernet standard uses all 4 pairs bidirectionally at 1 Gbps over UTP with a 100 m max?
questions.explanation: 1000BASE-T (Gigabit Ethernet) runs 1 Gbps over UTP with 100 m max and uses all 4 pairs bidirectionally.

questions.question: Which statement best describes full-duplex operation?
options: Device can send and receive data simultaneously on separate wire pairs without collisions
Device cannot send and receive at the same time; must wait
Data flows in only one direction at a time, determined by CSMA/CD
Device uses a single wire pair shared for both directions
questions.explanation: Full-duplex means a device can send and receive data simultaneously on separate wire pairs without collisions.

questions.question: What standards body defines Ethernet?
questions.explanation: Ethernet is a collection of network protocols and standards defined in IEEE 802.3.

questions.question: What does Auto MDI-X do?
options: Lets modern devices auto-detect and adjust transmit/receive pins, removing the need for crossover cables
Automatically assigns an IP address to an interface
Detects the duplex mode of the far-end device
questions.explanation: Auto MDI-X is a feature that lets modern devices auto-detect and adjust transmit/receive pins, removing the need for crossover cables.

questions.question: Which connector is the 8-pin registered-jack used on copper Ethernet cables?
questions.explanation: The RJ-45 is the 8-pin registered-jack connector used on copper Ethernet cables.

questions.question: What is a byte?
questions.explanation: A byte is 8 bits. A bit is the smallest unit of data (a 0 or 1).
